content: Who created the heavens and the earth?
explanation: Genesis 1:1 — 'In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.' This is the opening verse of the entire Bible.

content: According to Genesis 1:27, in whose image did God create mankind?
explanation: Genesis 1:27 — 'So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him.'

content: What did God use to form the first man?
explanation: Genesis 2:7 — 'The LORD God formed the man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living creature.'

content: What part of Adam did God use to create Eve?
explanation: Genesis 2:21-22 — 'The LORD God took one of his ribs... and made into a woman.'

content: Which animal tempted Eve to eat the forbidden fruit?
explanation: Genesis 3:1 — 'Now the serpent was more crafty than any other beast of the field that the LORD God had made.'

content: According to Genesis 3:6, who ate the forbidden fruit?
explanation: Genesis 3:6 — 'She took of its fruit and ate, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate.' Both ate the fruit.

content: Where in Genesis is the first promise of the Messiah (Protoevangelium) found?
options: 3:15 — when God speaks to the serpent
explanation: Genesis 3:15 — 'I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and her offspring; he shall bruise your head.' This is regarded as the first prophecy of Jesus.

content: What did Cain offer to God?
explanation: Genesis 4:3 — 'Cain brought to the LORD an offering of the fruit of the ground.' Abel brought firstborn of the flock.

content: Whom did Cain kill?
explanation: Genesis 4:8 — 'Cain rose up against his brother Abel and killed him.'

content: Which figure in Genesis did not experience death but was taken by God?
explanation: Genesis 5:24 — 'Enoch walked with God, and he was not, for God took him.' Hebrews 11:5 confirms Enoch did not see death.

content: How old was Methuselah when he died?
explanation: Genesis 5:27 — 'Thus all the days of Methuselah were 969 years, and he died.' He is the longest-lived person in the Bible.

content: What kind of wood did God tell Noah to build the ark with?
explanation: Genesis 6:14 — 'Make yourself an ark of gopher wood.'

content: How many pairs of clean animals did Noah take into the ark?
explanation: Genesis 7:2 — 'Take with you seven pairs of all clean animals, the male and his mate.' Unclean animals: only one pair.

content: How long did the rain of the great flood last?
explanation: Genesis 7:12 — 'And rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.'

content: Noah's ark came to rest on the mountains of ___.
explanation: Genesis 8:4 — 'In the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.'

content: What did the dove bring back to Noah after the flood?
explanation: Genesis 8:11 — 'The dove came back to him in the evening, and behold, in her mouth was a freshly plucked olive leaf.'

content: What sign did God use as a covenant with Noah that He would never again destroy the earth by flood?
explanation: Genesis 9:13 — 'I have set my bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.'

content: Whose descendants did Noah curse for the incident after the flood?
explanation: Genesis 9:25 — 'Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.' Noah cursed Canaan, son of Ham.

content: How is Nimrod described in the earth?
explanation: Genesis 10:9 — 'He was a mighty hunter before the LORD.'

content: Why did people build the Tower of Babel?
options: As a residence for the king
To make a name for themselves and not be scattered
explanation: Genesis 11:4 — 'Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be dispersed over the face of the whole earth.'

content: Abram's family set out from the city of ___ of the Chaldeans.
explanation: Genesis 11:31 — 'Terah took Abram his son... and they went forth together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan.'

content: To what land did God call Abram to leave his country?
explanation: Genesis 12:1,5 — God called Abram to leave his homeland for Canaan, the land He would show him.

content: How old was Abram when he left Haran?
explanation: Genesis 12:4 — 'Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.'

content: What land did Lot choose when separating from Abram?
explanation: Genesis 13:11 — 'Lot chose for himself all the Jordan Valley.' This land was as well watered as the garden of the LORD.

content: What did Melchizedek, king of Salem, bring out to meet Abram after the battle?
explanation: Genesis 14:18 — 'Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine. He was priest of God Most High.' A type of Christ in Hebrews 7.

content: God promised Abram's offspring would be as numerous as what?
explanation: Genesis 15:5 — 'Look toward heaven, and number the stars... So shall your offspring be.' The promise of stars appears here; sand of the sea is mentioned in 22:17.

content: How long did God say Abram's offspring would be sojourners and slaves?
explanation: Genesis 15:13 — 'Your offspring will be sojourners in a land that is not theirs and will be servants there, and they will be afflicted for four hundred years.'

content: Hagar bore Abram a son named what?
explanation: Genesis 16:15 — 'Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.'

content: God changed Abram's name to ___.
explanation: Genesis 17:5 — 'No longer shall your name be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham, for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations.'

content: What was the sign of the covenant between God and Abraham?
explanation: Genesis 17:11 — 'You shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.'

content: How did Sarah react when she heard the promise that she would bear a son?
explanation: Genesis 18:12 — 'Sarah laughed to herself, saying, After I am worn out, and my lord is old, shall I have pleasure?'

content: How many righteous persons did Abraham ask God to spare Sodom for?
explanation: Genesis 18:32 — Abraham asked from 50 down to 10 righteous people. The final number he asked was ten.

content: How did God destroy Sodom and Gomorrah?
explanation: Genesis 19:24 — 'Then the LORD rained on Sodom and Gomorrah sulfur and fire from the LORD out of heaven.'

content: What did Abraham say Sarah was while in Gerar?
explanation: Genesis 20:2 — 'Abraham said of Sarah his wife, She is my sister.' King Abimelech sent and took Sarah.

content: What was the name of Abraham and Sarah's son?
explanation: Genesis 21:3 — 'Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore him, Isaac.'

content: Whom did God test Abraham by telling him to offer as a sacrifice?
explanation: Genesis 22:2 — 'Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering.'

content: When Abraham was about to offer Isaac, God provided a ___ instead.
explanation: Genesis 22:13 — 'Abraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram, caught in a thicket by his horns. And Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.'

content: In which cave did Abraham bury Sarah?
explanation: Genesis 23:19 — 'Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah.'

content: Where did Abraham's servant meet Rebekah?
explanation: Genesis 24:13 — 'Behold, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.' The servant prayed and met Rebekah at the well.

content: Who became Isaac's wife?
explanation: Genesis 24:15,67 — Abraham's servant met Rebekah at the well, and she became Isaac's wife.

content: How old was Abraham when he died?
explanation: Genesis 25:7 — 'These are the days of the years of Abraham's life, 175 years.'

content: Of Isaac's twin sons, who was born first?
explanation: Genesis 25:25-26 — 'The first came out red... so they called his name Esau. Afterward his brother came out... and his name was called Jacob.'

content: What did Esau sell his birthright to Jacob for?
options: A bowl of red lentil stew
explanation: Genesis 25:34 — 'Then Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew, and he ate and drank and rose and went his way. Thus Esau despised his birthright.'

content: Whose wells did Isaac re-dig that the Philistines had stopped up?
explanation: Genesis 26:18 — 'Isaac dug again the wells of water that had been dug in the days of Abraham his father, which the Philistines had stopped after the death of Abraham.'

content: Who helped Jacob deceive Isaac to receive the blessing meant for Esau?
explanation: Genesis 27:6-17 — Rebekah devised the plan for Jacob to wear Esau's clothes and goat skins on his hands to deceive Isaac into giving him the blessing.

content: What did Jacob dream of at Bethel?
explanation: Genesis 28:12 — 'And he dreamed, and behold, there was a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven. And behold, the angels of God were ascending and descending on it!'

content: How many years did Jacob have to work to marry Rachel?
explanation: Genesis 29:18,20 — 'Jacob loved Rachel. And he said, I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.'

content: What is the name of Rachel's firstborn son?
explanation: Genesis 30:23-24 — 'She conceived and bore a son... and she called his name Joseph.' Benjamin was Rachel's second son.

content: What did Rachel steal from Laban when Jacob left?
explanation: Genesis 31:19 — 'Rachel stole her father's household gods.' These were the teraphim, household idols.

content: Jacob wrestled with the angel through the night by which river?
explanation: Genesis 32:22-24 — Jacob took his family across the ford of the Jabbok, then was left alone and wrestled a man until daybreak.

content: After wrestling with the angel, Jacob's name was changed to ___.
explanation: Genesis 32:28 — 'Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed.'

content: When Esau met Jacob again after many years, what did he do?
explanation: Genesis 33:4 — 'But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.'
options: Embraced and kissed Jacob's neck; both wept

content: Which two sons of Jacob slaughtered the men of Shechem to avenge Dinah?
explanation: Genesis 34:25 — 'Two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and came against the city while it felt secure and killed all the males.'

content: At Bethel, God confirmed Jacob's new name as what?
explanation: Genesis 35:10 — 'Your name is Jacob; no longer shall your name be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name.'

content: Of which nation is Esau the ancestor?
explanation: Genesis 36:1 — 'These are the generations of Esau (that is, Edom).' Esau settled in the hill country of Seir and became the ancestor of the Edomites.

content: What gift did Israel (Jacob) give Joseph that made his brothers jealous?
explanation: Genesis 37:3 — 'Israel loved Joseph more than any other of his sons... And he made him a robe of many colors.'

content: In Joseph's second dream, what bowed down to him?
options: Seven cows and seven ears of grain
The sun, the moon, and eleven stars
explanation: Genesis 37:9 — 'Behold, the sun, the moon, and eleven stars were bowing down to me.' The sun and moon represent his parents.

content: To whom did Joseph's brothers sell him?
explanation: Genesis 37:28 — 'They drew Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit, and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty shekels of silver.'

content: Of the twin sons of Judah and Tamar, who reached out his hand first but was born second?
explanation: Genesis 38:28-30 — Zerah's hand came out first, the midwife tied a scarlet thread, but Perez was born first. Perez is in the lineage of Jesus (Matthew 1:3).

content: Whom did Joseph serve as steward when he came to Egypt?
explanation: Genesis 39:1 — 'Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, had bought him.' Joseph was entrusted to manage Potiphar's house.

content: Why was Joseph thrown into prison in Egypt?
explanation: Genesis 39:17-20 — Potiphar's wife falsely accused Joseph of trying to violate her, so Potiphar put Joseph in prison.
options: He was falsely accused by Potiphar's wife

content: In prison, for whom of Pharaoh's officials did Joseph interpret dreams?
options: The priest and the storehouse keeper
explanation: Genesis 40:1-2 — 'The cupbearer of the king of Egypt and his baker committed an offense.' Joseph interpreted dreams for both in prison.

content: What did Pharaoh's dream foretell would happen in Egypt?
explanation: Genesis 41:29-30 — 'There will come seven years of great plenty throughout all the land of Egypt, but after them there will arise seven years of famine.'
options: Seven years of plenty followed by seven years of famine

content: How old was Joseph when he stood before Pharaoh to interpret the dream?
explanation: Genesis 41:46 — 'Joseph was thirty years old when he entered the service of Pharaoh king of Egypt.' David also began his reign at age 30 (2 Samuel 5:4).

content: When Joseph's brothers returned to Canaan, whom did Joseph keep as hostage?
explanation: Genesis 42:24 — 'He took Simeon from them and bound him before their eyes.' Joseph kept Simeon, not Reuben.

content: At Joseph's feast for his brothers, how much greater was Benjamin's portion than the others'?
explanation: Genesis 43:34 — 'Portions were taken to them from Joseph's table, but Benjamin's portion was five times as much as any of theirs.'

content: Joseph had his silver ___ hidden in Benjamin's sack of grain.
explanation: Genesis 44:2 — 'Put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest.' This was a plan to test his brothers.

content: When Joseph revealed his identity to his brothers, how did he respond?
explanation: Genesis 45:5 — 'Do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.'
options: Told them not to grieve, for God had sent him to preserve life

content: To what region of Egypt did Joseph invite his family to live?
explanation: Genesis 45:10 — 'You shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near me, you and your children and your children's children, and your flocks, your herds, and all that you have.'

content: How many people of Jacob's family in total went down to Egypt?
explanation: Genesis 46:27 — 'All the persons of the house of Jacob who came into Egypt were seventy.' This number 70 is also mentioned in Exodus 1:5.

content: How many years did Jacob live in Egypt?
explanation: Genesis 47:28 — 'And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years. So the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were 147 years.'

content: When blessing Joseph's two sons, on whose head did Jacob place his right hand?
explanation: Genesis 48:14 — 'Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Ephraim, who was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh.' Jacob deliberately favored the younger.

content: Why did Reuben lose his birthright privilege?
explanation: Genesis 49:3-4 — 'You went up to your father's bed; then you defiled it.' This refers to 35:22 when Reuben lay with Bilhah, Jacob's concubine.

content: Of which tribe did Jacob prophesy: 'The scepter shall not depart from... until Shiloh comes'?
explanation: Genesis 49:10 — 'The scepter shall not depart from Judah... until Shiloh comes.' A prophecy of the Messiah coming from the tribe of Judah.

content: Joseph said to his brothers: 'You meant evil against me, but ___ meant it for good.'
explanation: Genesis 50:20 — 'As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.'

content: On the first day of creation, what did God say?
explanation: Genesis 1:3 — 'And God said, Let there be light, and there was light.'
options: Let there be sea and land

content: On which day did God create the sun, moon, and stars?
explanation: Genesis 1:14-19 — The lights in the expanse of the heavens were created on the fourth day.

content: What did God bless mankind to do?
explanation: Genesis 1:28 — 'Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it.'
options: Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth

content: Where did the LORD God plant a garden?
explanation: Genesis 2:8 — 'And the LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east.'

content: What fruit did God forbid Adam to eat?
explanation: Genesis 2:17 — 'But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.'
options: The tree of the knowledge of good and evil

content: What does Genesis 2:24 teach about marriage?
explanation: Genesis 2:24 — 'Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife, and they shall become one flesh.'
options: Leave father and mother, hold fast to wife, become one flesh
Form a family but keep old patterns

content: After driving Adam from the garden, what did God place to guard the way to the tree of life?
explanation: Genesis 3:24 — 'He placed at the east of the garden of Eden the cherubim and a flaming sword that turned every way to guard the way to the tree of life.'

content: How many years did Adam live?
explanation: Genesis 5:5 — 'Thus all the days that Adam lived were 930 years, and he died.'

content: After the flood, God established a law: whoever sheds the blood of man, what is the punishment?
explanation: Genesis 9:6 — 'Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed, for God made man in his own image.'
options: His blood shall also be shed (death penalty)

content: What does the LORD promise to Abram in Genesis 12:3 about all the families of the earth?
explanation: Genesis 12:3 — 'And in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.'
options: They will be scattered far from him
They will be blessed in him

content: When Lot was captured, how many trained men of his household did Abram lead to rescue him?
explanation: Genesis 14:14 — 'When Abram heard that his kinsman had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, 318 of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.'

content: When God promised Sarah would bear a son, what did Abraham do?
explanation: Genesis 17:17 — 'Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed and said to himself, Shall a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old?'
options: Fell on his face and laughed inwardly

content: While fleeing Sodom, what did Lot's wife do that caused her transformation?
explanation: Genesis 19:26 — 'But Lot's wife, behind him, looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.'
options: Returned to the city for her belongings
Looked back and became a pillar of salt

content: What did Abraham name the place where he nearly offered Isaac, meaning 'The LORD will provide'?
explanation: Genesis 22:14 — 'So Abraham called the name of that place, The LORD will provide [Jehovah-Jireh].'

content: What did the LORD tell Rebekah about the older and younger of her twins?
explanation: Genesis 25:23 — 'Two nations are in your womb... the older shall serve the younger.'
options: The older shall rule the younger
The older shall serve the younger

content: After his ladder dream, Jacob named the place Bethel, which means what?
explanation: Genesis 28:19 — 'He called the name of that place Bethel,' meaning 'House of God.'

content: How did Laban deceive Jacob on his wedding night?
explanation: Genesis 29:25 — 'In the morning, behold, it was Leah! And Jacob said to Laban, What is this you have done to me?' Jacob then served seven more years to marry Rachel.
options: Sent a horse instead of a person
Gave Leah in place of Rachel

content: What does the place where Jacob wrestled God — called Peniel — mean?
explanation: Genesis 32:30 — 'So Jacob called the name of the place Peniel, saying, For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life has been delivered.'
options: Face of God (I have seen God face to face)

content: As Rachel died in childbirth, she named her last son Ben-oni; but what name did his father give him?
explanation: Genesis 35:18 — 'And as her soul was departing (for she was dying), she called his name Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.'

content: What did Onan, son of Judah, do that the LORD put him to death for?
explanation: Genesis 38:9-10 — 'But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his. So whenever he went in to his brother's wife he would waste the semen on the ground, so as not to give offspring to his brother. And what he did was wicked in the sight of the LORD, and he put him to death also.'
options: Refused to raise up offspring for his dead brother (levirate duty)

content: After interpreting the dream, what did Pharaoh give Joseph as a sign of high authority?
explanation: Genesis 41:42 — 'Then Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Joseph's hand and clothed him in garments of fine linen and put a gold chain about his neck.'
options: His signet ring, fine linen, and a gold chain

content: When the silver cup was found in Benjamin's sack, who offered to remain as a slave in his place?
explanation: Genesis 44:33 — 'Now therefore, please let your servant remain instead of the boy as a servant to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.' Judah made the offer.

content: When Pharaoh asked Jacob his age, what did he answer?
explanation: Genesis 47:9 — 'And Jacob said to Pharaoh, The days of the years of my sojourning are 130 years.'

content: In the blessing for Joseph (Gen 49:24), what name does Jacob use for God?
explanation: Genesis 49:24 — 'But his bow remained unmoved; his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob (from there is the Shepherd, the Stone of Israel).'
options: Mighty One of Jacob / Stone of Israel

content: Before he died, what did Joseph make his brothers swear about his bones?
explanation: Genesis 50:25 — 'Then Joseph made the sons of Israel swear, saying, God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.'
options: Bury him in Egypt at once
Carry his bones to the promised land when God visits them

content: According to Genesis, how many years did Sarah live after giving birth to Isaac (compare Gen 17:17 and 23:1)?
explanation: Gen 17:17 — Sarah was 90 when she heard the promise and bore Isaac at 91. Gen 23:1 — Sarah lived 127 years. Therefore she lived about 36-37 years after Isaac's birth.

content: Before God established the covenant of circumcision with Abraham (Gen 17), how many times had He promised the land of Canaan to him and his descendants?
explanation: Gen 12:7 (first, at Shechem), 13:14-17 (second, after Lot's separation), 15:18 (third, the covenant between the pieces) — three times before Gen 17.

content: Jacob came to Bethel twice. According to the text, why did he go there the second time (Gen 35)?
explanation: Gen 35:1-7 — God commanded Jacob to go up to Bethel and build an altar; he also told his household to put away foreign gods. The first visit (28:19) was while fleeing Esau and dreaming of the ladder.
options: By God's command, to build an altar and remove foreign gods
To flee from the massacre at Shechem
To meet his brother Esau again
To bury his beloved wife Rachel

content: Which of the following 'robes/garments' are explicitly mentioned in Joseph's story (Gen 37-50)? (select all correct)
explanation: Gen 37:3 — the robe of many colors made by his father; 39:12 — the garment Potiphar's wife seized as he fled; 41:42 — the fine linen robe given by Pharaoh when he was made governor. The 'gold-embroidered brocade for aged Jacob' is not in the text.
options: The robe of many colors made by his father
The garment Potiphar's wife seized as he fled outside
The fine linen robe Pharaoh gave when making him governor
A gold-embroidered brocade Joseph offered to aged Jacob

content: The LORD's prophecy to Rebekah while pregnant (Gen 25:23) is directly fulfilled in Isaac's blessing on Jacob (Gen 27:29) in which specific detail?
explanation: Gen 25:23 — 'the older shall serve the younger'. Gen 27:29 — 'your brothers shall bow down to you'. Both texts converge on the older serving/bowing to the younger.
options: The promised land of Canaan belongs to Jacob
Jacob's descendants will be as numerous as stars
Jacob is directly blessed by the LORD
Brothers will bow down and serve Jacob

content: Among the barren matriarchs, which one is the one whose husband is explicitly recorded as 'prayed to the LORD' on her behalf, and the LORD answered?
explanation: Gen 25:21 — 'Isaac prayed to the LORD for his wife, because she was barren. And the LORD granted his prayer.' With Sarah (16-21), Abraham is not recorded as praying directly. With Rachel (30:22), the text says 'God remembered Rachel', not that Jacob prayed.

content: In Genesis, patriarchs sometimes presented their wives as 'sister'. Which of these cases ARE in the text? (select all correct)
explanation: Gen 12:10-20 — Abram told Pharaoh Sarai was his sister; Gen 20:1-18 — Abraham told Abimelech of Gerar; Gen 26:6-11 — Isaac told Abimelech of Gerar. Jacob never did this; he stayed with Laban, Rachel's uncle.
options: Abram told Pharaoh that Sarai was his sister (Gen 12)
Abraham told Abimelech that Sarah was his sister (Gen 20)
Isaac told Abimelech that Rebekah was his sister (Gen 26)
Jacob told Laban that Rachel was his sister at Paddan-aram

content: According to Genesis 29-30, the following sons of Jacob were borne by Leah herself, not by her maidservant. Which cases are correct? (select all correct)
explanation: Gen 29:32-35 — Leah bore Reuben (firstborn), Simeon, Levi, Judah (later Issachar, Zebulun). Dan and Naphtali were the sons of Bilhah, her maidservant (30:5-8), not Leah.
options: Reuben was the firstborn borne by Leah herself
Dan was a son borne directly by Leah herself
Simeon was the second son borne by Leah herself
Naphtali was a son borne directly by Leah herself

content: Among the dreams recounted in Genesis, which one features 'eleven stars, the sun and the moon' bowing down?
explanation: Gen 37:9 — Joseph's second dream as a youth. Pharaoh dreamed of 7 cows and 7 ears of grain (41); the cupbearer dreamed of 3 vine branches (40); the baker dreamed of 3 baskets of bread (40).
options: Pharaoh's dream of seven lean cows

content: Which of the following covenant signs is NOT established by God in Genesis?
explanation: The rainbow (Gen 9:13) — covenant with Noah. Circumcision (Gen 17:11) — covenant with Abraham. The Sabbath is a covenant sign in Exod 31:13, not in Genesis. The pillar at Bethel (28:18) is a memorial pillar, not a universal covenant sign.
options: The rainbow set in the clouds

content: Genesis 7:16 explicitly states that 'the LORD shut him in'. Which theological truth does this detail emphasize in the flood narrative?
explanation: Gen 7:16 — the LORD himself shut the door, not Noah. This action affirms that both salvation (sealing those inside for protection) and judgment (decisively shutting out those outside) belong to God's sovereign authority.
options: Noah was too weak to close the ark door himself
The door was too large; God had to help close it
It was a signal that the flood rains were beginning
Both salvation and judgment come from the LORD, not man

content: According to the angel of the LORD's own words after Abraham obeyed in offering Isaac (Gen 22:12), what is the chief purpose of the test?
explanation: Gen 22:12 — 'Now I know that you fear God, seeing you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.' The test was to demonstrate (to all generations) Abraham's fear of God.
options: To demonstrate that Abraham truly fears God
To teach Abraham a lesson in humility
To prove humans cannot fully trust God

content: In Genesis 50:20, Joseph tells his brothers: 'You meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring about that many people should be kept alive.' Which theological truth does this express?
explanation: Gen 50:20 — a classic statement of providence: God reigns over all events, turning human evil schemes into the means of saving His people, without excusing the wickedness of those who plotted evil.
options: God can only use good people
Human sin does not deserve judgment
Every human action is God's direct will
God sovereignly turns evil into good for His people

content: Genesis 32:28 explains the name 'Israel' with what specific meaning according to the one wrestling with Jacob?
explanation: Gen 32:28 — 'Your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel, for you have striven with God and with men, and have prevailed.' The other names: 'father of many nations' = Abraham (17:5); 'God laughs/he laughs' = Isaac.
options: He who has striven with God and with men and has prevailed
Father of many nations across the whole earth by promise
One whom God has remembered in his deepest hour of need
God laughed when giving him his name at his birth

content: Abraham's dialogue bargaining with the LORD over Sodom (Gen 18:23-33) reveals which truth about God's character?
explanation: Gen 18:25 — 'Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?' This passage affirms that God is both perfectly just and patient enough to listen, willing to spare the city for the sake of the righteous. Abraham reduced the number from 50 to 10.
options: God is hesitant and easily persuaded by men to change His mind
God is perfectly just and willing to spare for the righteous' sake
God judges entirely at random without principle
God is indifferent to how many righteous live in the city

content: According to the ESV, Genesis 1:2 reads: 'The earth was without form and void, and darkness was over the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was ___ over the face of the waters.' Which word fills the blank?
explanation: Gen 1:2 (ESV) — 'And the Spirit of God was hovering over the face of the waters.' The Hebrew 'merachepheth' (hovering/brooding) is translated 'hovering' in ESV.

content: Genesis 3:14 (ESV) records the curse on the serpent: 'On your ___ you shall go, and ___ you shall eat all the days of your life.' How are the blanks filled in order?
explanation: Gen 3:14 (ESV) — 'on your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.' The image emphasizes the serpent's humiliation and shame after tempting Eve.

content: According to Genesis 6:3 (ESV), the LORD declared that man's days shall be how many years?
explanation: Gen 6:3 — 'My Spirit shall not abide in man forever, for he is flesh; his days shall be 120 years.' This is the warning that precedes the flood judgment.

content: Genesis 4:15 (ESV) — the LORD put a mark on Cain. According to the text itself, the purpose of the mark was that anyone finding Cain should NOT ___.
explanation: Gen 4:15 — 'And the LORD put a mark on Cain, lest any who found him should attack him.' The mark was a means of protection, showing the LORD's mercy even to the one who killed his brother.
options: recognize the guilt visible on his face
speak with him in pity or compassion
grant him asylum in their own land
attack and kill him in revenge for Abel

content: According to the words of the people of Shinar themselves in Genesis 11:4, what is the specific purpose of building a tower 'with its top in the heavens'?
explanation: Gen 11:4 — 'Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be dispersed over the face of the whole earth.' The answer is in their own words.
options: To draw near and worship God in heaven
To 'make a name' for themselves, lest they be dispersed
To offer sacrifices to the stars above
